title: TrustForge Bridges Registry
description: Per-deployment registry that overrides the default credential-resolver mapping. The daemon reads `.tf/bridges.yaml` once at startup, validates against this schema, and uses `resolveByIssuer` to map an incoming credential's issuer / iss claim / SPIFFE trust domain to a TrustForge bridge entry. When no entry matches, the resolver falls back to the built-in defaults declared in `tools/tf-daemon/src/credential-resolver.ts` (B2).

registry_version.description: Version of the bridges-registry schema itself.

default_profile.description: Optional conformance profile name applied when an entry omits its own. Purely informational at this layer; the FeatureGate is the authoritative gate.

bridges.description: Per-issuer bridge entries. The first entry whose `issuer_match` / `iss_pattern` matches an incoming credential wins.

kind.description: Which TrustForge bridge module handles this credential format.

issuer_match.description: Exact match against the credential's `iss` claim (OAuth/JWT) or trust-domain authority. Use `iss_pattern` for prefix/suffix matching.

iss_pattern.description: Substring match against the credential's `iss` claim. `clerk.dev` matches both `https://api.clerk.dev/...` and `clerk.dev`.

trust_domain.description: Trust domain the resolved actor belongs to.

trust_level.description: Initial TrustForge trust level assigned to actors resolved through this bridge entry.

capability_map.description: Mapping from the credential's native scope/permission to a TrustForge action name. Keys are the native scope (e.g. OAuth `email`); values are TrustForge action names matching the dotted ActionName pattern.
